zh.lifeSummary: 當Reverend Walter Covey於1798年9月5日出生在美國紐約州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩時，他的父親James Covey25歲，母親Phebe Smith21歲。他於1817年1月29日與Mary Winn在美國紐約州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在美國紐約州哈利素米特住了大約10年且在美國紐約州德拉沃梅雷迪思住了大約5年。他去世於1862年6月1日在美國紐約州奧瑟格米爾福德 特享年63歲，埋葬在美國紐約州奧瑟格米爾福德米爾福德米爾福德 特 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Reverend Walter Covey于1798年9月5日出生在美国纽约州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩时，他的父亲James Covey25岁，母亲Phebe Smith21岁。他于1817年1月29日与Mary Winn在美国纽约州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他在美国纽约州哈利素米特住了大约10年且在美国纽约州德拉沃梅雷迪思住了大约5年。他去世于1862年6月1日在美国纽约州奧瑟格米爾福德 特享年63岁，埋葬在美国纽约州奧瑟格米爾福德米爾福德米爾福德 特 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Covey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened form of MacCovey, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cobhthaigh (see Coffey ).

English: unexplained. Covey is used of ‘pantry’ in the early modern period and covey ‘little chap’ (a diminutive of cove, Middle English cof ‘bold, eager, courageous’; see Cove ) in the early nineteenth century, but there is no evidence to confirm either as the source for the surname.

variants.name: Coffey
meanings: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cathbhadha ‘descendant of Cathbhadh’, a byname meaning ‘battle tent’, Ó Cathbhuadhaigh ‘descendant of Cathbhuadhach’ (‘battle victorious’), or Ó Cathmhogha ‘descendant of Cathmhugh’ (‘battle slave’).
shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cobhthaigh ‘descendant of Cobhthach’, a byname meaning ‘victorious’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

en.lifeSummary: When Reverend Walter Covey was born on 5 September 1798, in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United States, his father, James Covey, was 25 and his mother, Phebe Smith, was 21. He married Mary Winn on 29 January 1817, in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Summit, Schoharie, New York, United States for about 10 years and Meredith, Delaware, New York, United States for about 5 years. He died on 1 June 1862, in Milford Center, Otsego, New York, United States, at the age of 63, and was buried in Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reverend Walter Covey est né le 5 septembre 1798, à New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, États-Unis, son père, James Covey, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Phebe Smith, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Mary Winn le 29 janvier 1817, à New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Summit, Schoharie, New York, États-Unis pendant environ 10 ans et à Meredith, Delaware, New York, États-Unis pendant environ 5 ans. Il est décédé le 1 juin 1862, à Milford Center, Otsego, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, New York, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Reverend Walter Covey am 5. September 1798 in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Covey 25 und seine Mutter Phebe Smith 21. Er hat am 29. Januar 1817 in New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Vereinigte Staaten Mary Winn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat ungefähr 10 Jahre lang in Summit, Schoharie, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und ungefähr 5 Jahre lang in Meredith, Delaware, New York, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 1. Juni 1862 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Milford Center, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend Walter Covey è nato il il 5 settembre 1798 nel New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, James Covey, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Phebe Smith, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Mary Winn il 29 gennaio 1817, nel New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Summit, Schoharie, New York, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 10 anni e nel Meredith, Delaware, New York, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 5 anni. Morì il 1 giugno 1862, nel Milford Center, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United: アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンニュー ボーティモーニュー ボーティモー
ja.Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Otsego: アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴミルフォードミルフォードミルフォード センター セメテリー

ja.lifeSummary: Reverend Walter Coveyが1798年9月5日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンニュー ボーティモーニュー ボーティモーで生まれた時，父James Coveyは25歳，母Phebe Smithは21歳であった。1817年1月29日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州グレエンニュー ボーティモーニュー ボーティモーでMary Winnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州スチオハーイスンミトに約10年間，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州デラワーメレディスに約5年間住んでいた。彼は1862年6月1日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴミルフォード センターで63歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴミルフォードミルフォードミルフォード センター セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United: 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 뉴 바팀어
ko.Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Otsego: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 미퍼드 미퍼드 미퍼드 세터 세멧리
ko.Milford Center, Otsego, New York, United: 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 미퍼드 세터
ko.lifeSummary: Reverend Walter Covey(이)가 1798년 9월 5일 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 뉴 바팀어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Covey(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Phebe Smith(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1817년 1월 29일 미국 뉴욕 주 그린 뉴 바팀어 뉴 바팀어에서 Mary Winn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 뉴욕 주 슈해리 수미에서 약 10년, 미국 뉴욕 주 데로르 메러디스에서 약 5년 동안 살았다. 그는 1862년 6월 1일 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 미퍼드 세터에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 미퍼드 미퍼드 미퍼드 세터 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reverend Walter Covey nasceu em 5 de setembro de 1798, em New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, James Covey, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Phebe Smith, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Winn em 29 de janeiro de 1817, em New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Summit, Schoharie, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América por quase 10 anos e em Meredith, Delaware, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América por quase 5 anos. Ele faleceu em 1 de junho de 1862, em Milford Center, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United: ню балтимор, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты
ru.Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Otsego: милфорд кентир кеметири, милфорд, милфорд, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты
ru.Milford Center, Otsego, New York, United: милфорд кентир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reverend Walter Covey родился 5 сентября 1798 г. в местности ню балтимор, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени James Covey было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Phebe Smith, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Winn 29 января 1817 г. в местности ню балтимор, ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности суммит, щохари, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет и в местности миредит, делавар, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты около 5 лет. Он умер 1 июня 1862 г. в местности милфорд кентир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности милфорд кентир кеметири, милфорд, милфорд, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

Se casó con Mary Winn el 29 de enero de 1817, en New Baltimore, New Baltimore, Greene, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Summit, Schoharie, Nueva York, Estados Unidos durante unos 10 años y en Meredith, Delaware, Nueva York, Estados Unidos durante unos 5 años. Murió el 1 de junio de 1862, en Milford Center, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Milford Center Cemetery, Milford, Milford, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
